We Hear This Every Week

You Shouldn't Have to Worry About Your Roof.

Here's how Benside handles the concerns homeowners bring to us most.

🔧 "My roof is leaking and I don't know how bad it is."

  • We get on the roof — not just look from the driveway
  • We check every penetration, flashing, valley, and pipe boot
  • We show you photos before we quote anything
  • Free. No obligation.

💰 "I don't know if I can afford this."

  • We tell you honestly: repair or replace
  • We don't upsell jobs that aren't necessary
  • Flexible financing available — no insurance required
  • Insurance claims handled start to finish

🏦 "I don't know what my insurance will cover."

  • We've navigated hundreds of Florida claims
  • We document damage the way adjusters require it
  • We know the difference between ACV and RCV policies
  • We push back when offers are too low

✅ "How do I know I can trust who I'm hiring?"

  • License #CCC1328155 — verify at myfloridalicense.com
  • Fully insured. Permits pulled and work code-inspected on every job
  • Work inspected by a licensed building inspector
  • Written contract before we touch anything

👷 "I don't want unknown people on my roof."

  • Our crew is our crew — not day labor
  • Same team Noel describes is the team that shows up
  • Noel is on-site or reachable throughout the job
  • Full accountability from start to finish

⏰ "I need this handled fast."

  • Same-day inspections when weather allows
  • Scheduling within days — not weeks
  • When we say Monday, we start Monday
  • You hear about any change from us first
What We Do

Every Roofing Service Your Home Needs — Done Right.

From tear-off to final inspection — we do it ourselves. Proper underlayment, correct flashing, the right nailing pattern. No shortcuts, no strangers on your roof.

🏗️
New Roof Installation

Full tear-off, new decking inspection, synthetic underlayment, proper drip edge and starter strip — every layer done right before a single shingle goes on. We install architectural shingle, standing seam metal, tile, and flat roofing systems. Every job is permitted, inspected, and built to Florida Building Code wind requirements.

Residential & Commercial
🔧
Roof Repair

Patch-on-patch doesn't fix a roof — it hides a problem until the next storm. We locate the actual source: failed pipe boot sealant, lifted flashing at a wall or chimney, opened valley seams, cracked ridge caps, or shingles that have lost their granule coating and are letting water in slowly. We fix what's causing it, not just what's visible.

Emergency & Scheduled
🌪️
Storm Damage & Insurance Claims

After a storm, don't let anyone on your roof before it's been properly documented — including a well-meaning neighbor with a tarp. We photograph every point of impact, measure affected areas, and write a scope of work your adjuster has to respond to line by line. Wind uplift, hail strikes, broken ridge caps, lifted flashing — we know exactly what insurers need to see.

Full Claim Support
🏠
Siding

Damaged or failing siding isn't cosmetic — it's how moisture gets into your wall cavity. In Florida's climate, that becomes mold and rot faster than most homeowners expect. We install and repair siding that actually protects your home from the outside in.

Residential & Commercial
🌧️
Gutters

Gutters protect your foundation. Clogged, failed, or improperly pitched gutters send water exactly where you don't want it — along your foundation, into your crawl space, and down your walls. We install and maintain gutter systems that actually work in Florida's heavy rain seasons.

Installation & Maintenance
🔨
Home Improvements

When you trust us with your roof, it makes sense to use us for other work on your home. Interior and exterior. One contractor you already trust, one point of accountability. We handle additional projects for homeowners who don't want to start the vetting process over again every time something needs attention.

Interior & Exterior
How It Works

What Happens When You Call Benside

No mystery, no high-pressure sales pitch. Here's the process — from the first call to the building inspection sign-off.

Step 01
📞 You Call or Fill Out the Form

We pick up or we call you back the same day. You talk to a real person — not an automated system, not a call center. We ask a few questions about what's going on with your roof and schedule your free inspection.

Step 02
🔍 Free On-Site Inspection

We get on the roof — not just look up at it from the driveway. We check the field of the roof, all penetrations, flashing at walls and valleys, ridge condition, soffit and fascia, and gutters. We photograph what we find and walk you through it before we write anything down. No charge. No obligation.

Step 03
📋 Written Estimate — No Surprises

You receive a detailed written estimate: materials, scope of work, labor, and timeline. Line by line. The price we quote is the price you pay — we don't discover "additional problems" once we're on your roof unless we show you evidence and you approve the change.

Step 04
🏦 Insurance Handled (If Applicable)

If your damage is storm-related, we coordinate with your adjuster directly. We've navigated hundreds of Florida insurance claims. You don't have to fight the insurance company alone — we know how this process works.

Step 05
🏗️ We Do the Work

Our crew starts on the date we committed to. We tear off properly, inspect the decking for rot or soft spots, install synthetic underlayment, set drip edge, and apply materials in the correct sequence. Every penetration gets sealed. Every valley gets flashed right. No shortcuts that fail on the first hard rain.

Step 06
✅ Final Walkthrough with You

Before we leave, we walk the job with you. You inspect the work. You ask questions. We don't collect final payment until you've seen the completed job and you're satisfied. That's how every job ends.

Day of Installation

What Happens the Day We Replace Your Roof

A lot of homeowners are nervous about what roof replacement actually looks like. Here's the real timeline.

7–8 AM

Crew Arrives & Setup

The crew arrives in the morning. Tarps go down around your home to protect landscaping, driveways, and AC units. If a dumpster is needed, it's already on site. We brief the team on the job scope before anyone climbs up.

Morning

Tear-Off

Existing roofing material is removed. Once we see the decking, we inspect every board. If we find rot or damage, we stop and show you before we proceed. You approve any changes before we add them to the scope.

Mid-Day

Underlayment & Installation Begins

Protective underlayment goes on first — this is what actually keeps your home dry if shingles ever get damaged. Then materials go on in the proper sequence. Flashing around penetrations, valleys, and edges is sealed correctly, not rushed.

Afternoon

Completion & Cleanup

Ridge caps, vents, and final flashing installed. Full magnetic sweep of your yard, driveway, and around the perimeter for nails. All material loaded and removed. Job site left clean — we don't leave a mess behind.

End of Day

Final Walkthrough

We walk the job with you before we leave. You see every piece of the work. You ask questions. Final payment only after you've signed off that you're satisfied. We hand you your written warranty documentation before we drive away.

If It Rains

What Happens With Weather

We monitor the forecast and plan around it. If rain forces us to pause, your roof is properly tarped and protected before we stop — we never leave open decking exposed. We'll communicate any schedule changes directly. We've been doing this in Florida long enough to know how to handle Florida weather.

Insurance & Financing

We Work With You — Insurance or Not.

Storm damage, wear and tear, or just time for an upgrade — Benside handles it all. You don't need an insurance claim to get a new roof. We offer three straightforward ways to get the job done.

🏠 3 Ways We Can Help You

  • ✔ Insurance Claim — Storm, wind, or hail damage? We guide you through the entire claim process and deal with your adjuster so you don't have to.
  • ✔ Financing — Wear and tear, aging roof, or no insurance coverage? No problem. We offer flexible financing so you can get it done now and pay over time.
  • ✔ Pay Directly — Have the budget ready? We'll get your roof done efficiently, on your schedule, no middleman involved.

Not sure which path fits your situation? Call us at (305) 910-6272 — we'll tell you exactly what makes the most sense for your roof.

How Benside Helps You

  • ✔ Complete photo documentation — every damaged area captured before anything is touched
  • ✔ Detailed written damage estimate — line by line, so your adjuster has to respond to every item
  • ✔ Direct adjuster communication — we attend the adjuster visit if needed and speak to the damage on your behalf
  • ✔ Supplemental claims support — if the first offer is too low, we know how to document and push back
  • ✔ We tell you what insurance won't cover — upfront, before we start, so there are no surprises

Storm claim windows close fast in Florida. Don't wait for the next rain to confirm what you already suspect. Call today and we'll inspect before your window closes.

📞 Call (305) 910-6272

🏠 No Insurance Claim? No Problem.

Many Florida homeowners replace aging or damaged roofs out of pocket — or simply want to upgrade to metal or tile. We offer flexible financing options so you don't have to put the whole job on yourself at once. Call us and we'll walk you through what works for your situation.

Get My Free Roof Assessment
Know What Goes On Your Roof

Roofing Materials — Explained in Plain Language

We recommend what works for your home, your climate zone, and your budget — not what's highest margin for us.

🏠
Asphalt Shingles
Lifespan: 20–30 Years

The most common roofing material in Florida. Good value, multiple styles, and when installed correctly with proper underlayment, holds up well to the Florida climate. We recommend 30-year architectural shingles for most residential homes — they're thicker, more wind-resistant, and look better than standard 3-tab.

Best Value Choice
⚡
Metal Roofing
Lifespan: 40–50+ Years

The premium choice for Florida homes. Metal handles hurricane-force winds better than asphalt, reflects heat (which lowers energy bills), and lasts decades longer. Higher upfront cost — but homeowners who plan to stay in their home long-term often find metal is the better investment over 20 years.

Best Long-Term Investment
🏛️
Tile Roofing
Lifespan: 30–50 Years

Concrete and clay tile are excellent in Florida's heat and hold up well in high winds. The classic Florida look with real durability. Important: tile is heavy — not every home's structure can support it without reinforcement. We assess your structure before recommending tile. Never install tile on a structure that wasn't designed for it.

Classic Florida Look
🏢
Flat Roofing (TPO / Modified Bitumen)
Lifespan: 15–25 Years

Flat roofs require different materials and different installation techniques than pitched roofs. TPO membranes, modified bitumen, and coating systems are the right choice for commercial buildings and many Florida home additions. These systems need proper drainage design and more maintenance than pitched roofs — but when done right, they perform.

Commercial & Additions

Meet the Owner

Noel Bendana — Owner, Benside Contractors Group

Noel Bendana
Owner & Director · Licensed Roofing Contractor

I started Benside because I spent years in this industry watching homeowners get taken advantage of. Contractors who disappeared after they cashed the check. Unlicensed crews who caused more damage than they fixed. Adjusters who paid pennies on the dollar because homeowners didn't know how to document what they had.

I was tired of it. So I started my own company with one rule that hasn't changed in over 25 years: I won't tell a homeowner they need something they don't need.

If your roof can be repaired, I'll tell you that even if a replacement is more profitable for me. If it needs to be replaced, I'll show you exactly why — photos, measurements, and a written explanation — before I quote you a number.

I'm reachable at (305) 910-6272 personally. And I stand behind every piece of work that leaves my company with a written warranty. I'm not the cheapest contractor you'll find. I'm the one who'll still answer your call two years after the job is done.

  • ✔ Florida Roofing Contractor License #CCC1328155 — verifiable at myfloridalicense.com
  • ✔ Fully licensed and insured — residential & commercial
  • ✔ 25+ years roofing in Florida's climate
  • ✔ Direct line: (305) 910-6272 — Noel answers personally

We Stand Behind Our Work

Written Warranty on Every Job

Any contractor who won't give you a warranty in writing is telling you something. We provide ours before work starts.

10
Year Workmanship Warranty

If your roof leaks because of how we installed it — poor flashing, improper sealing, incorrect overlap — we fix it at no charge. For 10 years. That's how confident we are in our work.

25–50
Year Manufacturer Warranty

Materials carry their own warranties — asphalt shingles typically 25–30 years, metal roofing often 40+ years. We explain your specific product warranty in writing before we start. You know what you're buying.

✔
In Writing Before We Start

You receive your warranty documentation before the crew touches your roof. Not after. Not "once the job is complete." Before we start. That's the standard. If a contractor won't put the warranty in writing upfront, walk away.

Making It Work For You

Financing Available for Qualified Homeowners

A roof replacement is a major expense. If you're paying out of pocket or your insurance coverage falls short, we work with financing options to help qualified homeowners get the job done without depleting savings.

We'll walk you through what's available when you call — no pressure, no obligation to use it. We just want to make sure cost isn't the reason a failing roof goes unaddressed.

✔ Flexible Payment Plans
✔ Quick Pre-Qualification
✔ No Pressure

Honest Answers

Questions Homeowners Ask Before They Call

If you have a question that isn't here, call us at (305) 910-6272.

We'll tell you honestly after we inspect it. If your structure is sound and the damage is isolated, a repair is the right call and that's what we'll recommend. We don't replace roofs that don't need replacing — and we don't patch roofs that are past their useful life just to buy a season and come back again. You get the real picture.
Call us first. We'll inspect and tell you whether the damage looks storm-related or like normal wear. If it's storm damage, we document it and help you file. We can't guarantee what your insurance pays — but we make sure every dollar of legitimate damage is documented so you're not leaving money on the table.
It depends on the size, materials, and what we find under the existing roof. No honest contractor can give you a real number over the phone — anyone who does is guessing. What we can do is come out, inspect, and give you a written estimate you can hold us to. The inspection is free.
Most residential replacements: one to three days depending on size and complexity. Repairs: typically same day or next day. We give you a timeline before we start and we stick to it. If anything changes, you hear about it from us — before you see it happen.
Go to myfloridalicense.com → Search CCC1328155 — you'll see Benside Contractors Group, the license status, and expiration date. We're not hiding anything. Every licensed contractor in Florida is in that database. Check before you hire anyone.
No. Our crew is our crew. We don't take your job and hand it off to a stranger you've never met. The team Noel describes to you is the team that shows up at your house. That's how we maintain quality and accountability on every job.
Call us at (305) 910-6272. We don't have a customer service department or a ticket system. The owner is your point of contact — before the job, during the job, and after. If something is wrong, we fix it.
Yes, we work with financing options for qualified homeowners. If cost is a concern, ask us when you call and we'll explain what's available. We'd rather find a way to help you get the job done than see a failing roof go unaddressed because of budget.
